title: Papaver delavayi
taxonomy: Papaver delavayi is classified within the family Papaveraceae. This family includes other species of poppies and related plants. Papaver delavayi is further classified within the genus Papaver, which includes approximately 50 species of poppies. The species Papaver delavayi is native to the mountainous regions of Yunnan and Sichuan provinces in China. It is a type of perennial poppy that grows in high-altitude meadows and rocky areas.

parts: Papaver delavayi. The medicinal parts of the Papaver delavayi, commonly known as Delavay's poppy or Himalayan poppy, include its roots, rhizomes, and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Papaver delavayi, also known as Delavay's poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer activities. The bioactive constituents of Papaver delavayi include alkaloids such as reticuline, protopine, and delavayine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are extracts and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
